summary.narrative: Gov. Braun won Indiana's open gubernatorial seat by record margin, campaigning on 'Freedom and Opportunity' platform. Early focus includes property tax relief, healthcare affordability, education reform, drug trafficking enforcement, and immigration restriction. Signed executive orders on health initiatives within first 100 days and advanced balanced budget protecting AAA credit rating.
summary.grade_rationale: Strong early execution on budget/fiscal management and executive actions. Limited legislative achievements documented yet. Property tax relief and school choice require sustained legislative effort. Healthcare and redistricting initiatives in progress but outcomes uncertain.

promises.normalized: Historic property tax relief through caps, deductions, transparency, and referendum reform
Make healthcare more obtainable and affordable for Indiana residents
Implement universal school choice and education reform programs
Strengthen drug trafficking enforcement and unauthorized immigration restrictions

data_gaps: Specific quantified targets for property tax relief absent
Healthcare affordability mechanisms not detailed publicly
Executive order details on health initiatives incomplete
Redistricting commitment timeline and specific actions undocumented
Drug enforcement penalty proposals not specified
